titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert Phelps to the Secretary of John Hopkin's Press, 19 January 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Robert Gunther Justin Phelps

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Robert Gunther Justin Phelps (b. 1876) served as librarian to the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland from 1897.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Baltimore, Maryland, United States of America

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
19th JANUARY 17. The Secretary,
Johns Hopkins Press,
Baltimore, Md. Dear Sir, In your letter of the 9th Dec., ult., I regret to find my
request for a copy of the October 1914 No., of the American
Journal of Insanity mentioned as a complaint indeed it
was no such thing. From the Editor of the Dublin Journal of
Med. Science this Library receivesa copy of the Journal and
the volumes are bound for reference. This being the only
Medical Library in Ireland where a file of the Journal can
be got for reference I hope that the Editor will grant a copy
of the issue so as to keep the file complete Trusting that you will understand that I was making no
complaint in this matter and will receive ny p apologies
for any annoyance caused, I remain, Dea Sir,
Yours faithfully,
Librarian.
